title: The War Of Art

jokes.text: Lisa wants a dot-dot-dot guinea pig?
jokes.setup: Homer reading Lisa's note requesting a guinea pig
jokes.punchline: Homer's confusion over Lisa's use of ellipsis/dot-dot-dot in her request
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's literal-minded confusion with modern communication conventions

jokes.text: And when they get excited, they jump straight up. It's called 'popcorning.' Lisa got to you. I don't know how, but she got to you.
jokes.setup: Marge sharing guinea pig facts with Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer's paranoid realization that Lisa has manipulated Marge through cute animal facts
jokes.explanation: Excellent character work showing Homer's awareness of Lisa's manipulation tactics and family dynamics

jokes.text: Emojis. Now she's gone too far.
jokes.setup: Homer discovering Lisa is using emojis in her communications
jokes.punchline: Homer treating emojis as a bridge too far in modern communication
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character consistency - Homer's resistance to modern technology

jokes.text: I don't want to be one of those girls who waits till she's 16 to decide if she's ready, and then it's too late.
jokes.setup: Lisa explaining her urgency about getting a pet
jokes.punchline: Lisa treating pet ownership with the language/urgency typically associated with losing one's virginity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversive humor applying adult relationship anxieties to childhood pet desires - very clever writing

jokes.text: I, Lisa Simpson, hereby promise to take full responsibility for this dog, cat, other. Circle 'other.'
jokes.setup: Reading formal pet contract language
jokes.punchline: The bureaucratic 'other' category for non-traditional pets
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor about bureaucratic language, but fairly predictable

jokes.text: If pet becomes Internet superstar, all rights revert to father.
jokes.setup: Reading more contract clauses
jokes.punchline: Homer's inclusion of viral pet video monetization rights in a child's pet contract
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Homer's get-rich-quick scheming applied to modern internet culture

jokes.text: The kid-parent contract. Unenforceable, yet you feel like you didn't completely cave.
jokes.setup: Homer reflecting on the pet contract
jokes.punchline: Homer's cynical but accurate assessment of symbolic parenting gestures
jokes.explanation: Sharp observational humor about parenting psychology with perfect economy

jokes.text: You know, most of these guys are rejects from the big guinea-pig mills in the Midwest.
jokes.setup: Pet shop worker explaining rescue guinea pigs
jokes.punchline: Treating guinea pig breeding like industrial puppy mills
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist concept but delivered by non-character, limiting integration

jokes.text: Oh, I can't tell you how many mill pigs we get in here who have bumblefoot or the slobbers.
jokes.setup: Continuing the guinea pig mill concept
jokes.punchline: Made-up diseases 'bumblefoot' and 'the slobbers' for guinea pigs
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay with silly disease names but feels forced

jokes.text: You don't have children, do you?
jokes.setup: Worker observing Lisa's intense emotional connection to the animals
jokes.punchline: Worker's deadpan assessment that this level of anthropomorphizing indicates childlessness
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery but somewhat predictable observation

jokes.text: She's gone, honey. She's gone.
jokes.setup: Lisa becoming overwhelmed by choice among guinea pigs
jokes.punchline: Homer treating Lisa's decision paralysis like a medical emergency or death
jokes.explanation: Great escalation humor treating minor indecision as major crisis

jokes.text: Maybe I should get that sick one. If I don't, no one else will.
jokes.setup: Lisa considering various guinea pigs
jokes.punchline: Lisa's bleeding heart liberalism applied to pet selection
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa character moment showing her social justice instincts in pet selection

jokes.text: I just wish the gray one had the brown one's personality.
jokes.setup: Lisa's continued indecision
jokes.punchline: Lisa projecting complex personalities onto guinea pigs she's known for minutes
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency but somewhat predictable pet selection humor

jokes.text: They tend to do better in pairs. I could get two?
jokes.setup: Worker mentioning guinea pigs do better in pairs
jokes.punchline: Lisa immediately considering doubling her already overwhelming decision
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure but predictable pet store scenario

jokes.text: Farting tweakers rule!
jokes.setup: Scene transition to Bart's activities
jokes.punchline: Bart's random outburst combining bodily functions and drug references
jokes.explanation: Random = funny approach with limited craft, though somewhat in character for Bart

jokes.text: Are you sure you didn't create a perfect habitat for you?
jokes.setup: Lisa describing elaborate guinea pig habitat with Peruvian elements
jokes.punchline: Marge suggesting Lisa designed it for herself rather than the pet
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about pet owner projection

jokes.text: I wish I could live in there. Well, there's plenty of room because Pokey's gone.
jokes.setup: Lisa admitting she wants to live in the habitat
jokes.punchline: The ironic reality that there's room because the pet has escaped
jokes.explanation: Nice ironic structure with good economy

jokes.text: This looks like a job for... fireplace tongs.
jokes.setup: Homer preparing to retrieve the guinea pig heroically
jokes.punchline: The mundane, practical tool rather than heroic solution
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection structure subverting superhero catchphrase expectations

jokes.text: No, that's a much bigger animal.
jokes.setup: Lisa hearing chewing sounds suggesting the guinea pig
jokes.punchline: Homer's matter-of-fact identification of a larger pest problem
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and economy, typical Homer nonchalance about household problems

jokes.text: Kettle corn. The heroin of the farmers' market.
jokes.setup: Homer eating kettle corn
jokes.punchline: Comparing trendy snack food to hard drugs
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy and quotability, perfect Homer food observation with drug comparison

jokes.text: It's just, it felt comforting to know that while we watched TV, there was art going on behind us.
jokes.setup: Marge explaining her attachment to the destroyed painting
jokes.punchline: The passive, ambient nature of art appreciation while TV watching
jokes.explanation: Brilliant observation about middle-class art appreciation - very Marge

jokes.text: Yeah, Milhouse played the violin for years until it turned out the vibrations were screwing up his bones.
jokes.setup: Discussion of musical instruments at yard sale
jokes.punchline: Pseudoscientific explanation for why Milhouse quit violin
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist medical claim typical of overprotective parenting

jokes.text: I've never seen a painting with a lighthouse before.
jokes.setup: Marge examining a lighthouse painting
jokes.punchline: Marge's inexperience with extremely common artistic subject matter
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Marge's limited art exposure

jokes.text: I didn't have a mirror, so I shaved in the reflection of the chrome frame.
jokes.setup: Kirk explaining his attachment to the painting
jokes.punchline: Pathetic details about his bachelor pad living conditions
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kirk moment - pathetic resourcefulness that's both sad and funny

jokes.text: You know it's rocking 'cause music notes are coming out of it.
jokes.setup: Homer describing a jukebox poster
jokes.punchline: Homer's literal interpretation of visual music note symbols
jokes.explanation: Good Homer literalness about visual art conventions

jokes.text: Well... to me, it looks like... garbage salad. But that's the great thing about art. Everyone can have their own opinion about why it sucks.
jokes.setup: Homer offering an art critique
jokes.punchline: Subverting art appreciation platitudes to conclude all art sucks
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection and character work - perfect Homer anti-intellectualism disguised as tolerance

jokes.text: I hate to think of the things this mirror has reflected.
jokes.setup: Marge removing the chrome frame from Kirk's bachelor pad painting
jokes.punchline: Marge's disgust at imagining Kirk's mirror activities
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Kirk's pathetic living situation with Marge's prudish reaction

jokes.text: It's not like it was painted by Leonardo Da Vinci Code.
jokes.setup: Homer dismissing the painting's potential value
jokes.punchline: Homer conflating Leonardo Da Vinci with Dan Brown's bestseller
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer malapropism combining high art with pop culture - very quotable

jokes.text: One: nudity, two: holograms, three: something terrible happening to Jesus.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining what makes art valuable
jokes.punchline: Homer's crude but oddly accurate categorization of valuable art themes
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer wisdom - crude but surprisingly insightful about art market realities

jokes.text: Nobody touch it! Bart, stop looking at it!
jokes.setup: Learning the painting is valuable
jokes.punchline: Marge's escalating protectiveness extending to mere visual contact
jokes.explanation: Great escalation humor and absurdist overprotectiveness

jokes.text: No, you'll wear it out!
jokes.setup: Bart wanting to look at the painting
jokes.punchline: Treating visual observation like physical deterioration
jokes.explanation: Good escalation continuation but slightly repetitive

jokes.text: Whoa, whoa, whoa, whoa, whoa, whoa, whoa, whoa, whoa! Whoa, whoa, whoa, whoa, whoa... whoa! Whoa, whoa, whoa, whoa, whoa, whoa, whoa. Whoa, whoa, whoa!
jokes.setup: Marge suggesting they split money with Van Houtens
jokes.punchline: Homer's excessive 'whoa' repetition showing extreme shock
jokes.explanation: Repetitive gag that goes on too long but captures Homer's greed shock

jokes.text: We can hang it on our wall, cut eyeholes in it to spy on people.
jokes.setup: Homer listing what they can do with the painting
jokes.punchline: Including vandalism for surveillance purposes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic combining art appreciation with sneaky schemes

jokes.text: If you think about it, aren't they really just the parents of a kid who happens to hang out with our kid?
jokes.setup: Homer questioning if Van Houtens are really friends
jokes.punchline: Reducing parental friendships to mere kid-based proximity
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about the artificial nature of parent social circles

jokes.text: Then they'll just be mad about the 65% that we're keeping.
jokes.setup: Marge suggesting giving Van Houtens 25%
jokes.punchline: Homer's math error while making his greedy point
jokes.explanation: Good character humor with Homer's bad math revealing his selfishness

jokes.text: like piranhas devouring a wounded yeti.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining how knowledge will eat away at the Van Houtens
jokes.punchline: Bizarre, over-the-top simile combining piranhas and mythical creatures
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd Homer simile - highly quotable and original imagery

jokes.text: No wonder Lisa's in love with you.
jokes.setup: Marge trying to manipulate Milhouse
jokes.punchline: Marge's inappropriate revelation about Lisa's feelings
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment showing Marge's desperation in negotiation

jokes.text: Bart agrees to promote and encourage use of my new cool nickname, 'C.J.'
jokes.setup: Milhouse negotiating terms for silence
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's pathetic attempt to rebrand himself with a cool nickname
jokes.explanation: Perfect Milhouse moment - pathetic social climbing attempt

jokes.text: You got it, Ceej.
jokes.setup: Bart agreeing to use Milhouse's nickname
jokes.punchline: Bart immediately making the nickname sound even less cool
jokes.explanation: Good immediate callback with Bart's characteristic ability to ruin things

jokes.text: I'm sorry, Mr. S. I cracked! We had a deal. And now we've got nothing!
jokes.setup: Milhouse confessing he told his parents
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's dramatic confession like a noir informant
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency with Milhouse's inability to keep secrets

jokes.text: I let Homer use our master bathroom!
jokes.setup: Kirk listing betrayals of trust
jokes.punchline: Bathroom access as the ultimate gesture of friendship
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kirk moment showing his misplaced sense of intimacy and privilege

jokes.text: We were gonna treat you to dinner at an upscale chain restaurant and never explain why, but now forget it!
jokes.setup: Marge threatening to withdraw planned generosity
jokes.punchline: The middle-class nature of their planned grand gesture
jokes.explanation: Excellent class-conscious humor and perfect Marge social awkwardness

jokes.text: You're so good at... collecting.
jokes.setup: Luann's sarcastic response about cell phone collection
jokes.punchline: Passive-aggressive dig disguised as compliment
jokes.explanation: Good passive-aggressive humor typical of suburban mom feuds

jokes.text: Sarcastic brr!
jokes.setup: Marge's reaction to Luann's coldness
jokes.punchline: Marge announcing the sarcastic nature of her own gesture
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-humor with Marge explaining her own sarcasm - highly quotable

jokes.text: I could write a check with today's date on it. That's the cushion.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining the benefits of financial security
jokes.punchline: Homer's simple, relatable definition of wealth
jokes.explanation: Excellent working-class observation about financial security - very relatable and quotable

jokes.text: So if you see the Simpsons walking down your street, lock your doors and hide... your friendship.
jokes.setup: Kent setting up dramatic warning about the Simpsons
jokes.punchline: Subverting physical danger warning into social ostracism advice
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection structure. Kent's dramatic pause subverting into social commentary works well for his character.

jokes.text: Great! Now we're raccoons, the masked bandits of the animal world!
jokes.setup: Homer reacting to negative TV coverage
jokes.punchline: Homer's animal metaphor for their current social status
jokes.explanation: Great Homer metaphor combining self-pity with animal knowledge

jokes.text: We don't even get this many Christmas cards.
jokes.setup: Marge observing the rocks thrown at their house
jokes.punchline: Comparing negative attention to their usual social isolation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor about social isolation with perfect Marge delivery

jokes.text: Just close your eyes and think of the cushion.
jokes.setup: Marge questioning if they're doing the right thing
jokes.punchline: Homer's parody of 'think of England' advice applied to greed
jokes.explanation: Good callback with sexual innuendo reference applied to financial motivation

jokes.text: While you two were separated, Kirk and used to be friends... with banana-fits.
jokes.setup: Dawn trying to explain her relationship with Kirk
jokes.punchline: Dawn's mispronunciation of 'benefits' as 'banana-fits'
jokes.explanation: Good malapropism humor revealing character's background and education level

jokes.text: Don't bother coming home, Kirk! Going once? Going twice? Sold! To the lonely auctioneer!
jokes.setup: Luann kicking Kirk out again
jokes.punchline: Auctioneer's sad self-deprecating auction call
jokes.explanation: Good juxtaposition of personal drama with professional duties

jokes.text: Sold! To the lonely auctioneer!
jokes.setup: Nobody wanting to give Kirk a ride
jokes.punchline: Auctioneer's self-aware joke about his own isolation
jokes.explanation: Decent self-deprecating humor from minor character

jokes.text: Oh, Luann, I'll never gaze from my thick eyeglasses into your thick eyeglasses ever again.
jokes.setup: Kirk lamenting his lost marriage
jokes.punchline: Kirk's unromantic description of their mutual vision problems
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kirk romance - unintentionally unromantic but sincere

jokes.text: Those parasailing guys take whatever they want.
jokes.setup: Kirk explaining how Dawn left with a parasailing instructor
jokes.punchline: Homer's matter-of-fact assessment of parasailing instructors as predatory
jokes.explanation: Great Homer observation treating vacation activity instructors as dangerous archetypes

jokes.text: That woman will say anything to keep herself in electric cigarettes.
jokes.setup: Kirk describing Dawn's motivations
jokes.punchline: Electric cigarettes as the new addiction/vice reference
jokes.explanation: Decent contemporary reference humor but somewhat dated now

jokes.text: He's been playing Dancing Revolution for hours, but the TV is off.
jokes.setup: Marge describing Milhouse's concerning behavior
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's psychological breakdown manifesting as imaginary video game playing
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor about childhood psychological stress with visual comedy element

jokes.text: That's a terrible thing for a father to do. That's why she'll believe it.
jokes.setup: Homer's cover story about wine tasting
jokes.punchline: His cynical parenting psychology
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about parenting expectations and believability

jokes.text: Three discos up, four gelatos on the left. You can't miss it. It's right between the disco and the gelato place.
jokes.setup: Tourist asking for directions
jokes.punchline: Directions that reveal the over-commercialized nature of the artistic community
jokes.explanation: Good satire of tourist destination commercialization

jokes.text: It's a liqueur made of fermented capers, the official drink of the island. Mmm! Sounds delicious. It's not.
jokes.setup: Server describing local drink
jokes.punchline: Server's honest admission that it tastes terrible
jokes.explanation: Good setup-punchline structure with honest service industry humor

jokes.text: Wrong! It's a me! A what? I painted it! Me, Klaus Ziegler!
jokes.setup: Homer identifying the painter as Oldenveldt
jokes.punchline: Dramatic revelation that it's a forgery with Mario-like 'It's a me!' delivery
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic revelation with Mario reference adding humor to exposition

jokes.text: 'Forger' is such a cruel word. I'm an art forger.
jokes.setup: Klaus being called a forger
jokes.punchline: Klaus's distinction without a difference
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay about professional dignity but somewhat predictable

jokes.text: The only real question to ask about art, whether it's in the Louvre or on a freshman's wall at Cal State Fullerton is, 'Did it move you?'
jokes.setup: Klaus defending his forgeries
jokes.punchline: Specific lowbrow college reference in artistic philosophy
jokes.explanation: Good philosophical point undermined by specific college reference

jokes.text: I'll pay you in Strupo.
jokes.setup: Homer asking for more forgeries
jokes.punchline: Offering the terrible local drink as payment
jokes.explanation: Good callback with Homer's typical poor negotiation skills

jokes.text: Though this fermented caper solution was originally used to dissolve seagull corpses, local alcoholics soon found Strupo to be a palatable drink of last resort.
jokes.setup: Documentary narration about Strupo
jokes.punchline: The progression from corpse disposal to alcoholic beverage
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor in fake documentary style - original and well-structured

jokes.text: The noxious liqueur causes powerful olfactory hallucinations, gender confusion, and wandering mouth.
jokes.setup: Continuing Strupo documentary
jokes.punchline: Escalating list of bizarre side effects ending with 'wandering mouth'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with absurd medical terminology, though 'wandering mouth' is the standout

tmdb_overview: After Lisa's new pet guinea pig destroys the Simpsons' living room art, Marge finds a beautiful painting at the Van Houten's yard sale, which Homer snaps up for 20 bucks
